Spoilers ahead, sort of an incomplete opinion, as I haven't really finished arc 2 ch4 yet.

|| Yk the morimens team really likes the lemurian stuff huh 

For me the arc 1 sonanil chapters were a fucking banger and prob the first time the story actually got interesting, and I was invested in the characters within the main plot of the game. I found its resolution to be good if a bit corny.
And a similar thing is happening now that Im about to finish arc 2 ch4, but I can't help feeling like it could be more.
I understand that, in order for the game logic to make sense, certain things have to happen, the keeper has to be the most powerful and bullshit ass they/them protagonist to have ever existed, but I feel that severely limits the potential of the stories Morimens tries to tell for its characters.
It makes sense in the regular world, where Murphy frees herself from the constraint of being the divine mother, for her story to have a silly haha hime archetype that just lazes around campus and is also your bestest friend uwu type conclusion, but I can't really fathom gmurphy's conclusion being in any way similar to that, even if for a moment, regardless of the fact that they are very much "the same person" (I would argue life experiences shape and change a person in a fundamental way, and living through the birth of a fake dead god should probably ruin a bit of the fun derived from simple pleasures, in a way it sort of does, but I would like for it to have a heavier toll on gmurphy's mental, and it not being equivalent to an almost eternal huff in the middle of the ocean, broken only by the extremely convenient intervention of protagonist mc power of friendship over here holy shit.)

I still like what they did, I really do, I just wish it had more of a lasting impact, in a similar manner to Ghelot or Gdoll.

Although this is honestly incredibly nitpicky, I reckon this is as good as it can get in terms of marrying the way the game works with its characters and stories. ||

^
Gushing about how cool the lemurian chapters tend to be in comparison to the rest of the arcs (BECAUSE I DONT CARE ABOUT ORPHANS AND I DONT CARE ABOUT LILY GET THESE KIDS OUT) as well as sort of complaining over silly stuff

Other opinions regarding the rest of the story, for me, not that anyone would care but I feel the need to get them out of my chest now that Im on it:

|| Aram and Thais was good, I like the sexualization being utilized in a way that makes sense
The ending of arc1 is messy and probably my second least favourite part of the entire story, with the first one being arc1  ch3-4, altho I understand it was necessary and also a really good segway into the first chapter of arc2. Clem is also cool and although her introduction is kinda random I like bug, I reckon her being less of a protagonist and more of a master of the maze menace is fitting and ok, I dont really NEED to know EVERYTHING about her or who she was previous to becoming bug, so the amount of info about her is honestly pretty well balanced

hm -- this is mostly because i'm thinking of writing a fic, but, what all do we know about the Church of the Lightbearers and more specifically how they work? like, the theology that they practice, what prayers they use, the significance of the lanterns in specific, that kind of thing? is there a compilation of those lore snippets anywhere, or?

hmm, off the top of my head,
1. theyre an offshoot of the chantry of life (salv event)
2. they have their own mythology of a "lightbearer who sacrificed their body" (pollux event?)
3. they have a tagline, "May the Lantern Shine Forever" (ch7 climax)
4. The title of Divus (pural is Divi) is not strictly for the twin god project. there's a divus named Eve that we havent met but is pretty involved outside the church (ch3, salv event)
iirc theres no specifics about their prayers that we know of yet

especially if i do it that way i want to and make all the factions as tabs in a single document (once again shooting myself in the ugc foot)

oh please do take care of yourself first, yes
if you do undertake such a project, i wish you luck! and that it is as painless as possible

(also -- i forget off the top of my head, but i *think* that the pollux audio drama also contains some of the lightbearer lore stuff? i don't entirely remember which bits are different from the story cutscenes in the pollux event though, not off the top of my head)
ever since pollux drama, i noticed the writers cut a few corners by using content from the respective feature story instead of original stuff
not that its a bad thing, mind you. some people prefer that since now it doesn't feel like an out of context scene

mmh, i did notice that the pollux audio drama shared a lot of stuff with story from the event while the castor audio drama was a whole original missing scene thing (just, with some slightly different wording between the audio drama and story stuff, for those scenes from the pollux audio drama)
i can definitely get why they'd do that, make it feel more integrated and probably saves money as well
as much as i do prefer the original VD scenes, i must admit it makes it easier to compile my info compared to it being scattered across different medias
